behaviors.description: Trim conversation history to the most recent 10 messages before sending to the LLM when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily request-construction wiring between conversation-state handling and the LLM call boundary. It does not require real infra or model-quality judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Create a history longer than 10 messages, invoke response generation with an LLM stub, and assert the payload sent to the LLM contains exactly the latest 10 messages in correct order.

behaviors.description: Ensure the generated response actually addresses the customer’s question and is not generic/off-topic.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly evaluates semantic quality/content of model output, which is model_judgment. Per guard, this is not mere API success.
behaviors.test_description: Run prompts representing concrete customer questions and score outputs against relevance criteria/rubric (or judge model). Fail if responses are generic or do not answer the asked question.

behaviors.description: Detect overly formal tone for casual-tier customers and trigger regeneration.
behaviors.justification: This combines output-quality judgment (tone appropriateness) with a conditional second-generation loop, making it multi_step_agentic. Rule order sets this to Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Provide cases where first draft is intentionally formal for a casual-tier customer, assert tone check flags it, verify a second LLM generation is invoked, and confirm final selected response is the regenerated one.

behaviors.description: Persist full exchange (user message, initial response, regenerations) with correct timestamps in the database.
behaviors.justification: This requires validating real persistence behavior and timestamp correctness against database state, which is real_infra.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a chat turn (including a regeneration path), then query the database and assert all exchange artifacts are stored with expected schema fields and chronologically correct timestamps.

behaviors.description: When regeneration is triggered, only the second response is surfaced to the customer; rejected draft is never shown.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-step control-flow/output-selection behavior over a regenerate path (multi_step_agentic), not just single-call wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Force tone-check failure on first draft, capture customer-facing output channel, and assert only regenerated response is emitted while first draft remains internal/logged only.
